title: tanacetum corymbosum subsp. clusii
intro: Tanacetum corymbosum subsp. clusii, also known as Clusius tansy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the chamomile family that is native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Tanacetum corymbosum subsp. clusii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe skin irritations. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
